pattern: Recording each visit order's cell position turns the whole check into confirming the tour starts at the corner and every consecutive pair of positions is a knight's-move apart.
hints: The tour must begin at the top-left cell, so the cell holding value 0 has to be at row 0, column 0 -- check that first.
Record which (row, column) holds each visit-order value so you can look up any two consecutive positions directly.
For every pair of consecutive visit orders k and k+1, the row difference and column difference between their cells must be exactly {1, 2} in some order -- that's the knight's-move test.

edge_inputs.description: valid full tour on a 5x5 board, starting at the corner
row-major numbering, no knight moves at all
minimum board size, a different invalid permutation
maximum board size with a genuine valid knight's tour from the corner
every consecutive move is a valid knight move, but the tour starts away from the corner
starts correctly at the corner but one single move partway through breaks the chain
6x6 board, valid nearly everywhere but one move near the end is broken

stress.description: random permutation grids of varying board size (mostly invalid, exercises the full scan)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(3, 7)
    vals = list(range(n * n))
    rng.shuffle(vals)
    grid = [vals[i * n:(i + 1) * n] for i in range(n)]
    return [grid]
